sanskrit:
क्रोधाद्भवति सम्मोहः सम्मोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः।
स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति॥
transliteration: krodhād bhavati sammohaḥ sammohāt smṛti-vibhramaḥ | smṛti-bhraṃśād buddhi-nāśo buddhi-nāśāt praṇaśyati ||
literal_meaning: From anger arises delusion; from delusion, memory-confusion; from memory-loss, intellect-loss; from intellect-loss, one perishes.
contextual_meaning: Anger hijacks the pre-frontal cortex, creating a neuro-cognitive cascade that erodes working memory, value clarity and self-monitoring, pulling the person back into compulsive, survival-level behavior.
psychological_state: Acute anger → dissociative fog → identity amnesia → moral disintegration → relapse into old patterns.
trigger_scenario: A recovering addict receives a humiliating text from an ex, flushes recovery plans, binges, and wakes up with self-loathing.
anchor_text: Pause before anger; one angry breath clouds memory, reason, and destiny.
